# Feature Specification: F-7 Mobile Top 10 Coverage Bundle

**Feature Branch**: `237-mobile-top-10-coverage-bundle`
**Created**: 2026-04-28
**Status**: Draft
**Input**: PRD: `docs/product/02_PRD/237-mobile-top-10-coverage-bundle-2026-04-28.md` (Approved 2026-04-28)
**Phase**: BLP-01 Tier 2 â€” second feature; first four-or-five-agent enrichment-branch execution.

---

## Overview

Close the OWASP Mobile Application Security Top 10:2024 detection-coverage gap in tachi by enriching four (or five) existing STRIDE-tier detection agents with **10 or 11 new Pattern Categories** drawn from M1â€“M10. F-7 is the fourth execution of the Heuristic A enrichment-branch protocol â€” extending F-3 single-agent (ASI07/`tool-abuse`), F-5 two-agent (LLM10/DoS+model-theft), and F-6 three-agent (ML01/03/04/06/07/08) precedents to four-or-five-agent fan-out. Net change is purely additive across 8 (single-host M8 path) or 10 (dual-host M8 path) of 28 detection-tier files plus one new ADR (ADR-036) and one new mutation-target example (`examples/mobile-banking-app/`); no new agent file, no schema bump, no orchestrator-dispatch edits, no consumers-list edit.

**Outcome**: After F-7 ships, all ten OWASP Mobile rows in the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix transition Planned â†’ Covered, lifting tachi's OWASP four-framework total from 30/40 to 40/40 â€” extending the AI-security framework family closure (LLM 10/10 + Agentic 10/10 + ML 10/10 post-F-6) to a fourth framework on a non-AI surface.

---

## User Scenarios & Testing *(mandatory)*

### User Story 1 â€” Mobile Threat Coverage on a Mobile-Banking Architecture (Priority: P1)

A security analyst threat-modeling a mobile-banking application architecture (mobile client + mobile-backend API + secure storage + third-party SDKs + biometric authentication) runs the tachi pipeline and receives Critical / High findings covering OWASP Mobile Top 10:2024 items M1â€“M10 alongside the web-tier and API-tier findings tachi already surfaces â€” without switching to a separate mobile-AppSec tool.

**Why this priority**: This is the primary user value of F-7. Today the analyst gets zero signal on mobile-specific threats from tachi; post-F-7, they get structured detection coverage across credentials, communication, storage, cryptography, IPC, supply chain, binary protections, and misconfiguration. P1 because every other story in F-7 is a structural enabler of this outcome.

**Independent Test**: Running tachi against `examples/mobile-banking-app/` produces â‰Ą10 new Mobile findings (â‰Ą1 per M1â€“M10) distributed across host agents per the canonical mapping in ADR-036. Verifiable by: (a) inspecting `threats.md` output for Mobile-category findings; (b) confirming each finding's `references:` array cites the appropriate `OWASP M{N}:2024` ID; (c) confirming each finding includes section-level MASTG/MASVS cross-references in the mitigation narrative.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** a mobile-banking architecture with credentials persisted in SharedPreferences (Android) instead of Android Keystore, **when** tachi runs the `spoofing` agent, **then** at least one new `S-{N}` finding emits citing `OWASP M1:2024 â€” Improper Credential Usage` with severity â‰Ą High and mitigation referencing platform-managed Keystore/Keychain with biometric-bound key release.
2. **Given** a mobile architecture with no certificate pinning declared in the mobile client, **when** tachi runs the `info-disclosure` agent, **then** at least one new `I-{N}` finding emits citing `OWASP M5:2024 â€” Insecure Communication` with severity â‰Ą High and mitigation referencing certificate pinning with backup-pin rotation and TLS 1.3 cipher allowlist.
3. **Given** a mobile architecture with an unencrypted SQLite database holding PII on the device, **when** tachi runs the `info-disclosure` agent, **then** at least one new `I-{N}` finding emits citing `OWASP M9:2024 â€” Insecure Data Storage` with severity â‰Ą High and mitigation referencing SQLCipher / Realm encryption with platform-keyring-derived keys.
4. **Given** a mobile architecture with a third-party SDK integrated without checksum verification or signed-artifact policy, **when** tachi runs the `tampering` agent, **then** at least one new `T-{N}` finding emits citing `OWASP M2:2024 â€” Inadequate Supply Chain Security` with severity â‰Ą Medium and mitigation referencing SDK signature verification and dependency manifest pinning with reproducible builds.
5. **Given** a mobile architecture with an exported Activity exposing sensitive functions without permission gating, **when** tachi runs the `tampering` agent, **then** at least one new `T-{N}` finding emits citing `OWASP M4:2024 â€” Insufficient Input/Output Validation` with severity â‰Ą Medium and mitigation referencing explicit Intent component routing and ContentProvider permission scoping.
6. **Given** a mobile architecture missing biometric step-up on money-movement operations, **when** tachi runs the `spoofing` agent, **then** at least one new `S-{N}` finding emits citing `OWASP M3:2024 â€” Insecure Authentication/Authorization` with severity â‰Ą Medium and mitigation referencing server-side authorization enforcement and biometric step-up on high-risk operations.
7. **Given** a mobile architecture missing root-detection in security-critical UI flows and exposing debug endpoints in production, **when** tachi runs the M8 host agent(s) (`privilege-escalation` and/or `repudiation` per ADR-036 D-numbered decision), **then** at least one new `E-{N}` and/or `R-{N}` finding emits citing `OWASP M8:2024 â€” Security Misconfiguration` with severity â‰Ą Medium and mitigation referencing production-build flag stripping and Play Integrity / DeviceCheck attestation gating.
8. **Given** the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix at `_internal/strategy/BLP-01-threat-coverage.md` Â§6, **when** F-7 is delivered, **then** all ten Mobile rows (M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, M6, M7, M8, M9, M10) transition from **Planned â†’ Covered**, with F-7 (Feature 237) named as the closure feature for all ten rows.

---

### User Story 2 â€” Four-or-Five-Agent Enrichment Without Architectural Surface Expansion (Priority: P1)

A tachi maintainer reviewing the F-7 PR diff sees exactly 8 (single-host M8 path) or 10 (dual-host M8 path) material file edits across host agents and companion catalogs plus one new ADR â€” and zero edits to schemas, orchestrator dispatch, the consumers list, or any other detection-tier file. The review confirms the Heuristic A enrichment-branch protocol scales cleanly to four-or-five-agent fan-out and the lean-agent tier caps from ADR-023 are preserved.

**Why this priority**: This is the protocol-stability outcome that unblocks Tier 3 features (F-8 Web/API). Without it, the maintainer cannot trust that further enrichment branches will not require schema bumps, new agents, or orchestrator-tier coupling. P1 because Tier 3 planning depends on this validation.

**Independent Test**: `git diff main HEAD -- '.claude/agents/tachi/' '.claude/skills/tachi-*/references/' schemas/` shows exactly 8 or 10 detection-tier files in the diff (with the M8-path determined by ADR-036's D-numbered decision), 0 lines changed in `schemas/`, 0 lines changed in `.claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md` and `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/dispatch-rules.md` and `finding-format-shared.md`, and all four-or-five modified agent files remain â‰¤120 lines.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** the post-F-6 detection-tier inventory of 28 files (14 host agents + 14 companion catalogs), **when** I review the F-7 PR, **then** exactly **8 files** (single-host M8 path) **or 10 files** (dual-host M8 path) are materially modified: `spoofing.md`, `tampering.md`, `info-disclosure.md`, plus M8 host(s) per ADR-036, plus the matching companions under `.claude/skills/tachi-{spoofing,tampering,info-disclosure,...}/references/detection-patterns.md`.
2. **Given** the line-cap rules in ADR-023 D1, **when** I check the modified agent files post-edit, **then** all four-or-five agents remain â‰¤120 lines (PRD baselines 51 + 55 + 54 + 52 + 50; expected post-edit â‰¤66 each; minimum margin â‰Ą54 lines).
3. **Given** the schema invariant for finding IDs, **when** I inspect `schemas/finding.yaml`, **then** `schema_version` remains `"1.8"` and `id.pattern` regex remains `^(S|T|R|I|D|E|AG|LLM|AGP|OI|MI|TE)-\d+$` â€” no new ID prefix added.
4. **Given** the 18-or-20-of-28-file zero-edit invariant on non-target detection-tier files, **when** I run `git diff main HEAD -- '.claude/agents/tachi/' '.claude/skills/tachi-*/references/'`, **then** exactly 8 or 10 files appear in the diff and the other 18 or 20 detection-tier files have zero byte changes.
5. **Given** the orchestrator-tier files (`orchestrator.md`, `dispatch-rules.md`, `finding-format-shared.md`), **when** I check the F-7 PR diff, **then** zero functional changes appear (annotation-only updates explicitly catalogued by architect at plan day; default is zero edits).
6. **Given** the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix ten-row transition, **when** the F-7 PR merges and ADR-036 transitions Proposed â†’ Accepted, **then** the public ADR ships with the canonical 10-or-11-row Mobile Top 10 sub-pattern â†’ owning-agent mapping table including the M4-vs-`output-integrity` disjoint-tells annotation and the Heuristic A four-or-five-agent consolidation rationale.

---

### User Story 3 â€” Byte-Identical Regeneration on Non-Mobile Baselines (Priority: P1)

A tachi adopter running the existing pipeline on an architecture without a mobile-platform topology sees byte-identical output before and after F-7 ships â€” no spurious new findings, no PDF page-count changes, no SARIF diffs. The adopter trusts the enrichment is fully scoped to mobile signals and produces zero false positives outside the mobile target surface.

**Why this priority**: Without byte-identity preservation, the F-7 enrichment would break the trust contract with adopters running tachi on non-mobile architectures (web apps, microservices, agentic systems, ML pipelines). P1 because any drift here invalidates the lean-agent inventory discipline that tachi's Tier 0 promise depends on.

**Independent Test**: `pytest tests/scripts/test_backward_compatibility.py -v` under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` passes 6/6 byte-identical against `security-report.pdf.baseline` for `web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference`. Mutation-target baselines (`agentic-app`, `consumer-agent-app`, `predictive-ml-app`, and the new `mobile-banking-app`) are explicitly excluded from the byte-identity loop.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** the 6 non-mutation-target example baselines, **when** I run `tests/scripts/test_backward_compatibility.py` under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021, **then** the test passes 6/6 byte-identical against `security-report.pdf.baseline` files.
2. **Given** the mobile-platform emission gate (new Pattern Categories fire only when mobile-platform topology indicators are present), **when** I check `threats.md` output for the 6 non-mobile baselines, **then** zero new mobile-tier findings appear: zero `S-{N}` for M1/M3, zero `T-{N}` for M2/M4/M7, zero `I-{N}` for M5/M6/M9/M10, zero `E-{N}`/`R-{N}` for M8.
3. **Given** the new `examples/mobile-banking-app/` mutation target, **when** I regenerate end-to-end, **then** â‰Ą10 new mobile-category findings emit (â‰Ą1 per M1â€“M10), and the regen produces an intentional baseline that is committed as the F-7 mutation-target precedent.
4. **Given** the structural-changes scope, **when** I review the F-7 PR diff, **then** zero changes appear in `scripts/`, `templates/`, `schemas/finding.yaml`, `schemas/taxonomy/owasp.yaml` (M1â€“M10 entries already present per F-A1 inventory), or any orchestrator dispatch table.

---

### Edge Cases

- **Non-mobile architecture under audit**: Pattern Categories' indicator gates reference mobile-platform-specific architectural tells (mobile client component, mobile-backend API declaration, mobile SDK reference, Keystore/Keychain mention, certificate pinning declaration, mobile permissions, package-name signal). Generic indicators (e.g., "API endpoint" or "data store") are explicitly excluded. On non-mobile architectures the new categories emit zero findings â€” preserving byte-identity per US-3.
- **MITRE ATT&CK Mobile catalog gap**: T1474 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1626 (Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism), T1398 (Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts) are NOT present in `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-attack.yaml` (verified at PRD time via grep). These citations appear as **prose-only** in `mitigation` narratives and Pattern Category Primary Source blocks, NOT in the structured `references` array. Mirrors F-5 (T1496 prose-only) and F-6 (3 of 6 ATLAS prose-only) precedent.
- **M8 split adjudication slip**: If architect cannot adjudicate the M8 dual-host vs single-host decision by Day 1 AM, fall back to single-host placement (M8 â†’ `privilege-escalation` only, encompassing both privilege-gain and accountability-loss variants in a single Pattern Category). F-7 still ships with no schema bump and no new agent; dual-host expansion becomes a follow-on no-schema-bump PR. PRD default and recommendation is dual-host.
- **Hybrid LLM-plus-mobile architecture**: M4 Insufficient Input/Output Validation has potential overlap with F-1's `output-integrity` agent (LLM-output sanitization). ADR-036 includes the M4 cross-agent boundary clarification: `tampering` Cat 12 owns mobile-IPC-input-side validation (deep-link parameters, Intent extras, URL-scheme parameters, exported ContentProvider gates); `output-integrity` owns LLM-output-side sanitization (LLM-generated content flowing into browser/SQL/shell sinks). Disjoint architectural-tells prevent duplicate emission on hybrid architectures.
- **Existing example with incidental mobile-keyword match**: `examples/microservices/architecture.md` mentions "End-user browser or mobile app" as a generic External Entity descriptor (one match across all 7 existing examples). This is NOT a structural mobile-platform topology indicator â€” no mobile-client process, no SharedPreferences/Keychain references, no SDK declarations. Pattern Category indicator gates require â‰Ą4 structural indicators per emission, ensuring incidental keyword matches on non-mobile baselines do not trigger findings.
- **MASTG/MASVS reference granularity**: All cross-references are at the **section-level** (e.g., `MASTG-AUTH`, `MASVS-CRYPTO`) per Q4 default, NOT at the test-case-ID level (`MSTG-AUTH-1`, `MSTG-CRYPTO-1`). Test-case-ID granularity is a F-A1 follow-on after MASTG IDs are added to a future taxonomy YAML file.

---

## Requirements *(mandatory)*

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-1**: System MUST extend `.claude/agents/tachi/spoofing.md` `owasp_references` list with `OWASP M1:2024 â€” Improper Credential Usage` and `OWASP M3:2024 â€” Insecure Authentication/Authorization`. Existing entries preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-2**: System MUST extend `.claude/agents/tachi/spoofing.md` `## Purpose` paragraph with 1â€“3 lines naming the mobile credential storage and mobile session handling surfaces. Pre-existing prose preserved byte-identically. Detection Workflow Step 5 references list extended with new OWASP Mobile + MASTG/MASVS citations. Agent file remains â‰¤120 lines (STRIDE tier cap per ADR-023 D1).
- **FR-3**: System MUST append two new Pattern Categories to `.claude/skills/tachi-spoofing/references/detection-patterns.md`: (a) Improper Mobile Credential Usage (M1) â€” covering credentials in SharedPreferences/NSUserDefaults/plaintext SQLite, hardcoded API keys in mobile binaries, credential leakage via clipboard/debug-logs/backups; (b) Insecure Mobile Authentication/Authorization (M3) â€” covering bypassed certificate pinning, broken/absent JWT validation, weak refresh-token handling, missing biometric step-up, client-side authorization decisions. Each Pattern Category includes â‰Ą4 indicators, â‰Ą1 worked example, OWASP Mobile primary citation, MASTG/MASVS related citations, â‰Ą3 specific mitigations. Existing 146 lines preserved byte-identically per ADR-023 D3.
- **FR-4**: System MUST extend `.claude/agents/tachi/tampering.md` `owasp_references` list with `OWASP M2:2024`, `OWASP M4:2024`, `OWASP M7:2024`. Existing entries (including F-6 ML01:2023 + AML.T0015) preserved byte-identically. `## Purpose` extended with 1â€“3 lines naming mobile SDK supply-chain integrity, mobile IPC input validation, and mobile binary protections surfaces. Detection Workflow Step 5 references extended. Agent file remains â‰¤120 lines.
- **FR-5**: System MUST append three new Pattern Categories to `.claude/skills/tachi-tampering/references/detection-patterns.md` after existing Pattern Category 10 (Adversarial Input Manipulation, Predictive ML â€” added by F-6): (a) Pattern Category 11 â€” Mobile Supply Chain Integrity (M2) â€” covering unsigned mobile SDK dependencies, sideloaded APK distribution, missing app-store provenance review, CocoaPods/Gradle/SPM unsigned sources; (b) Pattern Category 12 â€” Mobile IPC Input Validation (M4) â€” covering Android Intent injection, iOS URL-scheme injection, deep-link parameter tampering, exported ContentProvider permission gaps, pasteboard injection (with disjoint-tells annotation referencing F-1 `output-integrity` agent boundary per ADR-036); (c) Pattern Category 13 â€” Insufficient Mobile Binary Protections (M7) â€” covering missing root/jailbreak detection, missing RASP/anti-tampering stubs, missing code obfuscation, debug symbols in production, missing emulator detection on payment flows. Each Pattern Category meets the same indicator/example/citation/mitigation requirements as FR-3. Existing 221 lines (post-F-6) preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-6**: System MUST extend `.claude/agents/tachi/info-disclosure.md` `owasp_references` list with `OWASP M5:2024`, `OWASP M6:2024`, `OWASP M9:2024`, `OWASP M10:2024`. Existing entries preserved byte-identically. `## Purpose` extended with 1â€“3 lines naming mobile transport security, mobile privacy controls, mobile secure storage, and mobile cryptography surfaces. Detection Workflow Step 5 references extended. Agent file remains â‰¤120 lines.
- **FR-7**: System MUST append four new Pattern Categories to `.claude/skills/tachi-info-disclosure/references/detection-patterns.md`: (a) Insecure Mobile Communication (M5) â€” covering cleartext HTTP from mobile clients, missing TLS pinning, downgrade attacks, weak TLS cipher acceptance; (b) Inadequate Mobile Privacy Controls (M6) â€” covering PII/PHI in device-local caches without expiry, telemetry SDKs without consent gating, clipboard exposure on sensitive fields, screenshot leakage on PII screens, over-broad permission requests; (c) Insecure Mobile Data Storage (M9) â€” covering unencrypted SQLite/Realm/Room, unencrypted KeyValue stores, cloud-backup leakage, external SD-card writes, world-readable permissions; (d) Insufficient Mobile Cryptography (M10) â€” covering weak KDF on PINs, custom-rolled crypto, hardcoded symmetric keys, insecure PRNG seeding, deprecated cipher suites. Each Pattern Category meets the same indicator/example/citation/mitigation requirements as FR-3. Existing 192 lines preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-8**: System MUST extend the M8 host agent(s) â€” `.claude/agents/tachi/privilege-escalation.md` and/or `.claude/agents/tachi/repudiation.md` per the M8 split decision recorded in ADR-036 (PRD-default: dual-host) â€” `owasp_references` list with `OWASP M8:2024 â€” Security Misconfiguration`. Existing entries preserved byte-identically. `## Purpose` extended with 1â€“3 lines naming the mobile-misconfiguration surface variant for that host's signal class. Detection Workflow Step 5 references extended. Agent file(s) remain â‰¤120 lines.
- **FR-9**: System MUST append M8 Pattern Category(s) to the M8 host companion catalog(s) per the M8 split decision recorded in ADR-036:
  - **Default (dual-host)**: (a) `.claude/skills/tachi-privilege-escalation/references/detection-patterns.md` gains "Mobile Security Misconfiguration â€” Privilege-Gain Variant (M8)" â€” covering exposed debug endpoints in production, default permissive ContentProvider/Service exports, missing app-attestation enabling tampered-binary execution, missing root-detection on security-critical features. (b) `.claude/skills/tachi-repudiation/references/detection-patterns.md` gains "Mobile Security Misconfiguration â€” Accountability-Loss Variant (M8)" â€” covering missing audit logging on security-relevant events, disabled crash reporting in production, debug logs leaking sensitive data via `Log.d`/`NSLog`, missing tamper-evident timestamping.
  - **Fallback (single-host)**: One Pattern Category encompassing both privilege-gain and accountability-loss variants on the chosen host (architect adjudication per R2 contingency).
  - Each Pattern Category meets the same indicator/example/citation/mitigation requirements as FR-3. Existing line counts (213 / 148) preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-10**: System MUST author `examples/mobile-banking-app/architecture.md` as the F-7 mutation target, exhibiting six mobile-platform topology indicators: (a) mobile client process (Android or iOS) handling user credentials; (b) credential-handling component using SharedPreferences/NSUserDefaults instead of platform-managed Keystore/Keychain; (c) secure-storage data store (encrypted or unencrypted SQLite on device); (d) mobile-backend API process; (e) third-party mobile SDK integration (analytics, payment, crash reporting); (f) at least one exposed debug or admin endpoint demonstrating M8 surface. Authoring effort: ~6â€“8 hours, architect drafts and senior-backend-engineer reviews/refines.
- **FR-11**: System MUST author `docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-036-mobile-top-10-coverage-bundle.md` under the Proposed â†’ Accepted dual-commit pattern (per ADR-027 / ADR-032 / ADR-034 / ADR-035 precedent). ADR-036 includes: (a) canonical 10-or-11-row Mobile Top 10 sub-pattern â†’ owning-agent mapping table with severity-hint annotation column; (b) Heuristic A enrichment-branch consolidation rationale at four-or-five-agent scope; (c) additive-only edit discipline conformance per ADR-023 D3; (d) explicit non-creation-of-new-agent rationale; (e) no-schema-bump symmetry with F-3/F-5/F-6; (f) M4 cross-agent boundary clarification with F-1 `output-integrity`; (g) M8 split decision (dual-host or single-host) with rationale; (h) zero-MAESTRO-reference invariant proof on the eight-or-ten enriched files; (i) cross-references to ADR-023 D3, ADR-030 D1, ADR-032, ADR-034, ADR-035 (with explicit citation of ADR-035 closing forward-scope marker forecast text).
- **FR-12**: System MUST update `_internal/strategy/BLP-01-threat-coverage.md` Â§6 Coverage Matrix with ten row transitions: M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, M6, M7, M8, M9, M10 â€” all Planned â†’ Covered. Closure-feature column populated with "Feature 237 (F-7)" for all ten rows. Coverage milestones panel updates to reflect OWASP Mobile Top 10:2024 = 10/10 Covered and OWASP four-framework total = 40/40 Covered.
- **FR-13**: System MUST preserve schema invariants. `schemas/finding.yaml` `schema_version` remains `"1.8"`. `id.pattern` regex remains `^(S|T|R|I|D|E|AG|LLM|AGP|OI|MI|TE)-\d+$` â€” no new prefix; `S`, `T`, `I`, `E`, `R` already enumerated. F-7 is the fourth BLP-01 detection feature with no schema bump (after F-3, F-5, F-6) and the first at four-or-five-agent enrichment scope.
- **FR-14**: System MUST preserve the orchestrator-tier zero-edit invariant. No functional changes to `.claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md`, `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/dispatch-rules.md`, or `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/finding-format-shared.md`. All four-or-five host agents already registered in dispatch-rules; no consumers-list edit needed.
- **FR-15**: System MUST verify byte-identity preservation via `tests/scripts/test_backward_compatibility.py` under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021 across 6 non-mutation-target baselines: `web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference`. Test passes 6/6 byte-identical against `security-report.pdf.baseline` files. Verification owner: `tester` agent (separate from `senior-backend-engineer` who authors edits) per F-3/F-4/F-5/F-6 separation-of-duties precedent.
- **FR-16**: System MUST update `tests/scripts/test_backward_compatibility.py` to add `mobile-banking-app` to the mutation-target exclusion list (alongside `agentic-app`, `consumer-agent-app`, `predictive-ml-app`) and update `DETECTION_AGENT_PATHS` and `DETECTION_PATTERN_REF_ENRICHMENT_HOSTS` frozensets per the four-or-five-host enrichment scope.
- **FR-17**: System MUST add F-7 enrichment-coverage tests at `tests/scripts/test_mobile_top_10_coverage_bundle_enrichment.py` covering: (a) line-cap verification on enriched agent files; (b) MAESTRO-grep zero-mention invariant; (c) MANDATORY Read directive presence; (d) Pattern Category Disambiguation subsections; (e) per-fixture references-array correctness for catalog-resolvable citations (OWASP Mobile, MASTG/MASVS sections); (f) prose-only fallback for non-catalog-resolvable MITRE ATT&CK Mobile entries (T1474, T1626, T1398).

### Key Entities

- **Pattern Category**: A markdown subsection within a `detection-patterns.md` companion catalog. Represents a class of architectural risks tied to a specific OWASP framework item. Attributes: H2 heading, description, â‰Ą4 architectural indicators, worked example(s), Primary Sources block (â‰Ą1 OWASP primary citation + â‰Ą1 related citation), Mitigations list (â‰Ą3 specific control mechanisms). 10 or 11 new Pattern Categories ship in F-7 (one per M1â€“M10, with M8 producing 1 or 2 depending on the M8 split decision in ADR-036).
- **Detection Agent**: An existing `.md` file under `.claude/agents/tachi/` that orchestrates threat-pattern matching for one signal class (e.g., spoofing, tampering, info-disclosure). F-7 enriches 4 (single-host M8) or 5 (dual-host M8) host agents additively â€” `owasp_references` extended, `## Purpose` extended, Detection Workflow Step 5 references extended. No new agents.
- **Companion Catalog**: An existing `detection-patterns.md` file under `.claude/skills/tachi-{agent-name}/references/`. Holds the Pattern Category catalog for one detection agent. F-7 appends 2/3/4/1-or-2 Pattern Categories to the spoofing/tampering/info-disclosure/M8-host companions respectively. No new companions.
- **Architectural Indicator**: A bullet item within a Pattern Category specifying a structural tell that, when present in a target architecture, gates finding emission. F-7 indicators are mobile-platform-specific (mobile client component, Keystore/Keychain mention, certificate-pinning declaration, mobile SDK reference, mobile permissions, package-name signal) â€” generic indicators are explicitly excluded to preserve byte-identity on non-mobile baselines.
- **ADR-036**: Public per-feature Architecture Decision Record at `docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-036-mobile-top-10-coverage-bundle.md`. Documents the canonical Mobile Top 10 sub-pattern â†’ owning-agent mapping table, the Heuristic A four-or-five-agent consolidation rationale, the M4 cross-agent boundary clarification, the M8 split decision, and cross-references to precedent ADRs (023, 027, 030, 032, 034, 035). Lifecycle: Proposed â†’ Accepted dual-commit per ADR-027.
- **F-7 Mutation-Target Example**: A new directory `examples/mobile-banking-app/` containing `architecture.md` exhibiting all six mobile-platform topology indicators. Authored at plan day (Wed 2026-04-29) by architect + senior-backend-engineer co-authoring track. Excluded from `tests/scripts/test_backward_compatibility.py` byte-identity loop alongside other mutation targets (`agentic-app`, `consumer-agent-app`, `predictive-ml-app`).
- **BLP-01 Coverage Matrix**: Strategy-tier table at `_internal/strategy/BLP-01-threat-coverage.md` Â§6 tracking row-by-row OWASP framework coverage status (Planned / Covered) and the tachi feature that closed each row. F-7 transitions ten rows (M1â€“M10) from Planned â†’ Covered.

---

## Success Criteria *(mandatory)*

### Measurable Outcomes

- **SC-1**: `.claude/agents/tachi/spoofing.md` `owasp_references` list extended to include `OWASP M1:2024` and `OWASP M3:2024`; existing entries preserved byte-identically. Agent file line count remains â‰¤120 (PRD baseline 51; expected post-edit 55â€“60).
- **SC-2**: `.claude/agents/tachi/spoofing.md` `## Purpose` section gains a 1â€“3 line extension naming the mobile credential storage and mobile session handling surfaces. Pre-existing `## Purpose` prose preserved byte-identically.
- **SC-3**: `.claude/skills/tachi-spoofing/references/detection-patterns.md` (146 lines pre-edit) gains two new Pattern Categories (M1, M3). Existing content preserved byte-identically per ADR-023 D3. Each new Category has â‰Ą4 indicators, â‰Ą1 worked example, â‰Ą1 OWASP Mobile primary citation, â‰Ą1 MASTG/MASVS related citation.
- **SC-4**: `.claude/agents/tachi/tampering.md` `owasp_references` list extended to include `OWASP M2:2024`, `OWASP M4:2024`, `OWASP M7:2024`; existing entries (including F-6 ML01:2023) preserved byte-identically. Agent file line count remains â‰¤120 (PRD baseline 55; expected post-edit 60â€“66).
- **SC-5**: `.claude/agents/tachi/tampering.md` `## Purpose` section gains a 1â€“3 line extension naming mobile SDK supply-chain integrity, mobile IPC input validation, and mobile binary protections surfaces. Pre-existing prose (including F-6 extension) preserved byte-identically.
- **SC-6**: `.claude/skills/tachi-tampering/references/detection-patterns.md` (221 lines post-F-6) gains three new Pattern Categories (M2, M4, M7) appended after existing Cat 10. Existing Categories 1â€“10 preserved byte-identically. Each new Category meets the same indicator/example/citation requirements as SC-3 (with M4 also citing the F-1 `output-integrity` disjoint-tells boundary annotation).
- **SC-7**: `.claude/agents/tachi/info-disclosure.md` `owasp_references` list extended to include `OWASP M5:2024`, `OWASP M6:2024`, `OWASP M9:2024`, `OWASP M10:2024`; existing entries preserved byte-identically. Agent file line count remains â‰¤120 (PRD baseline 54; expected post-edit 60â€“66).
- **SC-8**: `.claude/agents/tachi/info-disclosure.md` `## Purpose` section gains a 1â€“3 line extension naming mobile transport security, mobile privacy controls, mobile secure storage, and mobile cryptography surfaces. Pre-existing prose preserved byte-identically.
- **SC-9**: `.claude/skills/tachi-info-disclosure/references/detection-patterns.md` (192 lines pre-edit) gains four new Pattern Categories (M5, M6, M9, M10). Existing categories preserved byte-identically. Each new Category meets the same indicator/example/citation requirements as SC-3.
- **SC-10**: M8 host(s) per ADR-036 D-numbered decision (`privilege-escalation` and/or `repudiation`) gain `OWASP M8:2024` in `owasp_references`; companion gains 1 or 2 new Pattern Categories (1 in single-host path; 1 per host in dual-host path). Existing entries preserved byte-identically. Agent file(s) remain â‰¤120 lines.
- **SC-11**: Primary Sources lists in all four (or five) companion files extended with the new OWASP Mobile Top 10:2024 citations applicable to that companion's host. Existing Primary Sources entries preserved byte-identically.
- **SC-12**: Agent invocation on the new `examples/mobile-banking-app/` architecture produces â‰Ą10 new findings (â‰Ą1 per M1â€“M10, â‰Ą11 if M8 splits across two agents). Distribution: â‰Ą2 new `S-{N}` from `spoofing` (M1, M3); â‰Ą3 new `T-{N}` from `tampering` (M2, M4, M7); â‰Ą4 new `I-{N}` from `info-disclosure` (M5, M6, M9, M10); â‰Ą1 (single-host) or â‰Ą2 (dual-host) new `E-{N}` and/or `R-{N}` from M8 host(s). Each new finding cites `OWASP M{N}:2024` primary in its `references:` array.
- **SC-13**: All 6 non-mobile baselines regenerate byte-identically under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021: `web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference`. `agentic-app`, `consumer-agent-app`, `predictive-ml-app`, and the new `mobile-banking-app` are excluded as mutation targets. Verification owner: `tester`.
- **SC-14**: Zero new runtime dependencies â€” empty diff on `pyproject.toml`, `requirements*.txt`, `package.json`. Zero new developer dependencies â€” `pyyaml` and `pytest` already declared.
- **SC-15**: Schema invariant preserved â€” `schemas/finding.yaml` `schema_version` remains `"1.8"`; `id.pattern` regex remains `^(S|T|R|I|D|E|AG|LLM|AGP|OI|MI|TE)-\d+$`. F-7 is the fourth BLP-01 detection feature with no schema bump (after F-3, F-5, F-6) and the first at four-or-five-agent enrichment scope. Outcome forecast by ADR-035 closing forward-scope marker.
- **SC-16**: Zero-edit invariant preserved on every detection-tier file other than the F-7 enrichment targets. Detection-tier inventory post-F-6 = 28 files (14 host agents + 14 companion catalogs). F-7 modifies 8 (single-host M8 path) or 10 (dual-host M8 path); adds 0 new files. Post-F-7 inventory remains 28. **20 of 28 files remain byte-identical in the single-host M8 path; 18 of 28 in the dual-host M8 path.** No `finding-format-shared.md` consumers list edit; no functional orchestrator/dispatch-rules edit.
- **SC-17**: Every emitted Pattern-Category-{N+1}+ finding carries the appropriate OWASP Mobile Top 10:2024 ID in its `references:` array (catalog-resolvable per `schemas/taxonomy/owasp.yaml`). MASTG/MASVS section-level cross-references appear in `mitigation` prose. MITRE ATT&CK Mobile entries (T1474, T1626, T1398) appear as **prose-only** in `mitigation` narratives â€” NOT in the structured `references` array (per Edge Case: catalog gap). F-7 does NOT extend `source_attribution` populator wiring (F-A3 deferral; one-way dependency).
- **SC-18**: BLP-01 Coverage Matrix transition: M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, M6, M7, M8, M9, M10 â€” all Planned â†’ Covered (ten rows). Coverage milestones panel updates to reflect OWASP Mobile Top 10:2024 = 10/10 Covered and OWASP four-framework total = 40/40 Covered (LLM 10/10 + Agentic 10/10 + ML 10/10 + Mobile 10/10).
- **SC-19**: Pipeline wall-clock latency on a mobile architecture increases by â‰¤7% relative to a pre-F-7 mobile-pipeline baseline (pattern-loading bounded; aggregate â‰¤500 lines added across all enriched companion files). PDF report regeneration on `mobile-banking-app/` completes in â‰¤2 minutes including infographic generation.
- **SC-20**: ADR-036 ships at `docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-036-mobile-top-10-coverage-bundle.md` under Proposed â†’ Accepted dual-commit pattern. Mandatory content present: canonical mapping table (10 or 11 rows + reference rows; severity-hint column populated), Heuristic A four-or-five-agent consolidation rationale, additive-only edit discipline conformance proof, explicit non-creation-of-new-agent rationale, no-schema-bump symmetry citation, M4-vs-`output-integrity` disjoint-tells annotation, M8 split decision (with rationale), zero-MAESTRO-reference invariant proof, cross-references to ADR-023 D3 / ADR-027 / ADR-030 D1 / ADR-032 / ADR-034 / ADR-035 (with explicit ADR-035 closing forward-scope marker citation).

## Assumptions

- **A1 â€” VERIFIED**: M1â€“M10 entries are present in `schemas/taxonomy/owasp.yaml` per F-A1 delivery (Feature 180, CLOSED). Verified at PRD time and re-verified at spec time via `grep "^- id: M[0-9]" schemas/taxonomy/owasp.yaml` returning 10 of 10 records.
- **A2 â€” VERIFIED**: F-A2 (`source_attribution` schema field; Feature 189, CLOSED) ships the data-shape contract. F-7 does not extend populator wiring (F-A3 deferred per ADR-028 D6; one-way dependency F-7 â†’ F-A3).
- **A3 â€” RESOLVED AT SPEC TIME** (PRD originally pending plan-day verification): MITRE ATT&CK Mobile entries T1474, T1626, T1398 are **NOT catalog-resolvable** â€” `grep "T1474|T1626|T1398" schemas/taxonomy/mitre-attack.yaml` returns zero matches. **Prose-only fallback applies** per F-5 (T1496 prose-only) + F-6 (3 of 6 ATLAS prose-only) precedent: these citations appear in `mitigation` narratives and Pattern Category Primary Source blocks ONLY, not in structured `references` arrays. ADR-036 codifies this rule per F-A2 referential-integrity contract.
- **A4 â€” VERIFIED**: Post-F-6 detection-tier file count is 28 (14 host agents + 14 companion catalogs). F-7 modifies 8 (single-host M8) or 10 (dual-host M8) and adds 0 net new files; post-F-7 inventory remains 28.
- **A5 â€” VERIFIED at spec time**: Empirical grep across `examples/*/architecture.md` for `android|ios|mobile|keystore|keychain|sharedpref|nsuser` returns one incidental match in `examples/microservices/architecture.md` (line: "End-user browser or mobile app" â€” generic External Entity descriptor, NOT a structural mobile-platform topology indicator). Zero structural mobile-platform components across all 7 existing examples. New `examples/mobile-banking-app/` authoring at plan day is the default path.
- **A6**: Pattern Category indicator gates require â‰Ą4 mobile-platform structural indicators per emission. Generic indicators (e.g., "API endpoint", "data store") are excluded. Incidental keyword matches on non-mobile baselines do not trigger findings â€” preserving SC-13 byte-identity.

## Plan-Day Decision Deferrals

These six questions from the PRD are **explicit deferrals** (not [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers â€” PRD provides comprehensive context and deferral defaults):

| ID | Question | PRD Default | Owner | Resolves At |
|----|----------|-------------|-------|-------------|
| Q1 | M8 split: single-host (`privilege-escalation` only) vs dual-host (`privilege-escalation` + `repudiation`)? | Dual-host (one Pattern Category per host with disjoint architectural-tells) | architect | plan.md / ADR-036 D-decision |
| Q2 | Mobile-banking-app vs alternative mutation-target archetype? | mobile-banking-app (covers credentials, payment, biometrics, secure storage, certificate pinning across all M-items) | product-manager + architect | plan.md FR-10 confirmation |
| Q3 | MITRE ATT&CK Mobile catalog-resolvability for T1474/T1626/T1398? | Resolved at spec time: prose-only fallback (verified zero catalog hits) | architect | already resolved |
| Q4 | MASTG/MASVS reference granularity: section-level vs test-case-ID level? | Section-level (e.g., `MASTG-AUTH`, `MASVS-CRYPTO`); test-case-ID is F-A1 follow-on | architect | plan.md |
| Q5 | ADR-036 mapping-table severity-hint column? | Yes (matches ADR-034 5-row + ADR-035 11-row precedent) | architect | plan.md / ADR-036 |
| Q6 | `mobile-banking-app/` post-F-7 baseline strategy: include in `BASELINE_EXAMPLES` or remain excluded as mutation target? | Excluded (matches `predictive-ml-app/`, `consumer-agent-app/`, `agentic-app/` precedent) | tester | tasks.md / Day 3 close-out |

In addition, the architect MEDIUM-2 deferral (ADR-036 9-decision vs 10-decision structure with Pattern Category Disambiguation as discrete D-numbered decision per ADR-035 D-9 precedent) and the team-lead MEDIUM-1/2 deferrals (plan-day overload mitigation via parallel `mobile-banking-app/` authoring track + Day 2 AM throughput via FR-7 task split into 4 sub-tasks for mid-morning checkpoint) are absorbed at the plan.md / tasks.md authoring tier per architect + team-lead explicit guidance in PRD v2.

---

## Out of Scope

**Could Have (P2) â€” Not in F-7**:
- MASVS verification levels (L1/L2/R) as per-finding tags â€” deferred to a follow-on; F-7 cites MASVS control categories as related references only.
- Platform-specific (Android-only or iOS-only) detection sub-patterns â€” F-7 covers cross-platform mobile patterns.
- MASTG test-case-ID cross-references on a per-finding basis â€” deferred until MASTG IDs are added to a future taxonomy YAML file.
- React Native / Flutter / Xamarin specific pattern variants â€” F-7 covers native Android/iOS patterns.

**Won't Have â€” Explicitly Excluded**:
- New `mobile-platform` agent file â€” violates Heuristic A consolidation per ADR-036 D-numbered decision (would fragment Mobile Top 10 across eleven locations).
- Schema bump on `schemas/finding.yaml` â€” no new ID prefix; S/T/I/E/R already in regex per F-A1.
- Orchestrator dispatch table edits (functional) â€” host agents already registered.
- `finding-format-shared.md` consumers list edit â€” host agents already in consumers list.
- `source_attribution` populator wiring extension â€” that scope is F-A3.
- MASTG taxonomy YAML file authoring â€” F-A1 follow-on scope, not F-7.
